record.field: ZITADEL is a cloud-native, open-source identity and access management platform that gives applications complete authentication, authorization and user management out of the box.

ZITADEL is a cloud-native, open-source identity and access management platform that gives applications complete authentication, authorization and user management out of the box.

Written in Go and running as a single binary on PostgreSQL, ZITADEL is built around event sourcing for a full, tamper-evident audit trail. It targets developers and enterprises that need production-grade SSO with first-class multi-tenancy, exposing gRPC and REST APIs so identity becomes a programmable part of the stack rather than a black box. It supports both centralized B2E setups and complex B2B/B2C scenarios through its Organizations and Projects hierarchy.

Key features
  • Standards-based auth: OpenID Connect (certified), OAuth 2.0, SAML 2.0 and SCIM 2.0
  • Passwordless and strong auth: FIDO2/WebAuthn passkeys, MFA, and external identity providers (LDAP, social and enterprise IdPs)
  • Infrastructure-level multi-tenancy with Organizations, Projects and fine-grained role-based access control
  • Hosted login UI plus fully programmable gRPC, ConnectRPC and REST APIs, webhooks and an Actions extensibility framework
  • Complete audit trail via event sourcing, enabling forensic visibility into every identity change
  • Cloud-native and Kubernetes-first: horizontal scaling, zero-downtime updates, Docker and Terraform support
Best for

Teams self-hosting a modern, API-driven identity provider for SaaS products, B2B platforms or internal SSO who want passkeys, multi-tenancy and audit compliance without building auth themselves. A strong open-source alternative to Keycloak, Auth0 and similar IAM services.

services: zitadel: restart: always image: ghcr.io/zitadel/zitadel:latest command: start-from-init --masterkey "{{ zitadel_masterkey }}" --tlsMode disabled environment: ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_HOST: db ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_PORT: 5432 ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_DATABASE: zitadel ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_USER_USERNAME: zitadel ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_USER_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_USER_SSL_MODE: disable ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_ADMIN_USERNAME: postgres ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_ADMIN_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} ZITADEL_DATABASE_POSTGRES_ADMIN_SSL_MODE: disable ZITADEL_EXTERNALSECURE: false ZITADEL_EXTERNALPORT: 8080 ZITADEL_EXTERNALDOMAIN: {{ external_domain }} ZITADEL_TLS_ENABLED: false ZITADEL_NOTIFICATIONS_SMTP_HOST: '' ZITADEL_NOTIFICATIONS_SMTP_PORT: '' ZITADEL_FIRSTINSTANCE_ORG_HUMAN_USERNAME: {{ zitadel_firstinstance_org_human_username }} ZITADEL_FIRSTINSTANCE_ORG_HUMAN_PASSWORD: {{ zitadel_firstinstance_org_human_password }} ZITADEL_FIRSTINSTANCE_ORG_HUMAN_EMAIL_ADDRESS: {{ zitadel_firstinstance_org_human_email_address }} ZITADEL_FIRSTINSTANCE_ORG_HUMAN_FIRSTNAME: {{ zitadel_firstinstance_org_human_firstname }} ZITADEL_FIRSTINSTANCE_ORG_HUMAN_LASTNAME: {{ zitadel_firstinstance_org_human_lastname }} ZITADEL_DEFAULTINSTANCE_FEATURES_LOGINV2_REQUIRED: false depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' volumes: - ./zitadel_data:/app/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: restart: always image: postgres:17-alpine environment: PGUSER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} POSTGRES_DB: zitadel volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready - -d - zitadel - -U - postgres interval: 10s timeout: 30s retries: 5 start_period: 20s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
